hero.title: Radiant Waves Media Late Night Global News
hero.description: Late-night roundup of major developments, security updates, political headlines and business news from around the world.

breaking: 🔴 Middle East tensions and diplomatic negotiations remain the world's top security story
🔴 Nigeria sustains military operations against terrorists, bandits and kidnapping networks
🔴 Peter Obi reiterates call for a political and legal resolution to the Nnamdi Kanu case
🔴 Defence Minister warns local informants continue aiding criminal and terrorist groups
🔴 Europe advances migration reforms and border security measures
🔴 United States and allies monitor global security and economic developments
🔴 China expands artificial intelligence and advanced manufacturing initiatives
🔴 Global markets remain cautious amid inflation concerns and geopolitical risks

featured.title: Security, Migration And Economic Uncertainty Dominate Global Headlines
featured.content: As the day comes to an end across much of the world, governments remain focused on security threats, migration challenges, economic reforms and geopolitical developments. International attention continues to center on the Middle East, European migration policies and security operations across Africa.

articles.title: Global Leaders Continue Diplomatic Engagement
articles.description: Governments and international organizations remain engaged in diplomacy aimed at maintaining stability, strengthening cooperation and addressing emerging security challenges.

articles.title: Middle East Security Situation Remains Global Focus
articles.description: Diplomatic contacts, regional security concerns and humanitarian issues continue shaping international discussions as governments seek pathways toward stability.

articles.title: United States Monitors Economic And Security Priorities
articles.description: American officials continue focusing on economic performance, trade, technology leadership, border security and international partnerships.

articles.title: Canada Continues Immigration And Housing Discussions
articles.description: Canadian policymakers remain focused on balancing immigration targets, workforce requirements and housing affordability challenges.

articles.title: Migration Policies Continue To Shape European Politics
articles.description: European governments continue debating asylum systems, deportation agreements and border security measures as migration remains a major political issue.

articles.title: European Security Cooperation Remains Strong
articles.description: NATO members continue strengthening intelligence sharing, military readiness and defense coordination amid ongoing regional concerns.

articles.title: Nigeria Intensifies Nationwide Security Operations
articles.description: Security agencies continue operations targeting insurgents, bandits and kidnapping syndicates as authorities seek to improve public safety across affected regions.

articles.title: Defence Minister Raises Alarm Over Local Informants
articles.description: Authorities say local informants continue providing support to terrorist and criminal organizations, posing significant challenges to security operations.

articles.title: Peter Obi Calls For Peaceful Resolution Of Nnamdi Kanu Matter
articles.description: The former Labour Party presidential candidate has renewed calls for a lawful and peaceful resolution of the Nnamdi Kanu case.

articles.title: Abdulsalami Clarifies Circumstances Surrounding MKO Abiola's Death
articles.description: Former Head of State Abdulsalami Abubakar stated that MKO Abiola collapsed during a meeting with visiting U.S. envoys and was not poisoned.

articles.title: Report Suggests Government May Expand eNaira Usage
articles.description: Discussions continue around the possible use of digital payment platforms, including the eNaira, for broader public sector transactions.

articles.title: Agricultural Stakeholders Report Fertilizer Supply Stability
articles.description: Industry officials credit early procurement efforts for maintaining stable fertilizer supplies during the current farming cycle.

articles.title: Asia Continues Technology And Manufacturing Expansion
articles.description: Asian economies remain focused on innovation, industrial growth and digital infrastructure investment.

articles.title: China Advances AI And Semiconductor Development
articles.description: Chinese authorities continue supporting artificial intelligence, semiconductor production and advanced manufacturing initiatives.

articles.title: Global Markets Monitor Inflation And Geopolitical Risks
articles.description: Investors continue assessing interest-rate expectations, energy prices and geopolitical developments affecting economic growth.

articles.title: Energy Markets Remain Sensitive To Global Events
articles.description: Oil and gas markets continue responding to international developments, supply forecasts and demand expectations.

breakingHistory: Middle East diplomacy and security concerns remained major global priorities.
Nigeria continued nationwide operations against terrorism, banditry and kidnapping.
Peter Obi renewed calls for a lawful resolution of the Nnamdi Kanu case.
Defence Minister warned that local informants are aiding terrorist operations.
Europe continued migration and border security reforms.
Canada addressed housing and immigration pressures.
China expanded AI and industrial modernization programs.
Global markets monitored inflation and geopolitical risks.
Agricultural stakeholders reported stable fertilizer supply conditions.
Governments focused on security, economic growth and long-term stability.
